rulings.Case Title: CHARLES SARVER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, February 10, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant 3M's motion for summary judgment is denied. There is a triable issue whether plaintiff wore the 8710 mask to protect himself from asbestos exposure. 3M's D'Amico objection is overruled. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BRUCE GLEASON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, February 10, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Textron Inc's motion to dismiss or stay for forum non-conveniens is denied. Textron has not shown that all defendants are subject to jurisdiction in Arizona. Unlike Hansen v. Owens-Corning Fiberglas Corp., 51 Cal.App.4th 753, plaintiff has not sued 200 defendants. Furthermore, many of the relevant events took place in California and several defendants are California companies. Plaintiff's request for monetary sanctions is denied as Textron's motion was made in good faith. Textron was not a "party affected by the order" at the time Ford's motion to dismiss was denied and therefore has the right to be heard on its own independent motion without compliance with the reconsideration statute. =(220/HEK)
